articles.title: Open Thread
articles.content:
登录
创建新账户
主要是法学教授 | 有时持不同意见 | 通常支持自由意志主义 | 始终保持独立
你在想什么？
尤金·沃洛克
|
2026年7月2日 凌晨3:00
下一篇：环境保护征用依然是征用
尤金·沃洛克是斯坦福大学胡佛研究所的托马斯·M·西贝尔高级研究员，同时也是加州大学洛杉矶分校法学院荣誉退休的加里·T·施瓦茨杰出法学教授和杰出研究教授。当然，他在这里的文章（就像其他博主的文章一样）仅代表个人观点，不代表任何机构的立场。他还与他人共同主持《言论自由不沉默》播客。

articles.title: Conservation Commandeering Is Still Commandeering
articles.content:
海滩照明可能对海龟造成问题。夜间明亮的灯光会使海龟幼体迷失方向，阻止它们爬向大海。充足的滨海滩涂照明甚至可能构成对红海龟和其他受《濒危物种法》保护的物种的禁止性“捕获”行为。

假设在海龟栖息地建造或维护滨海滩涂照明属于《濒危物种法》第9条规定的“捕获”行为，那么州或地方政府允许此类滨海滩涂照明的决定——无论是通过许可还是仅仅未能禁止——是否也构成第9条下的“捕获”？如果是这样，《濒危物种法》是否要求州或地方政府对在海龟繁殖期间建造或维护滨海滩涂照明的私人土地所有者采取行动？这样的要求是否符合宪法？我认为答案是否定的。

我认为，根据《濒危物种法》追究州和地方政府的责任——无论是对私人行为发放许可证和许可，还是未能加以控制——都违反了反劫持原则，或者正如我在即将发表于《天主教大学法律评论》专刊的文章《保护性劫持》中所论证的那样。尽管如此，法院经常以这种方式让州和地方政府承担替代责任，这通常是对环保组织根据《濒危物种法》提起的公民诉讼作出的回应。相关案件涉及州机构未能对毛皮捕猎或捕鱼活动施加足够严格的限制等问题。在本文中，我注意到美国第十一巡回上诉法院有一桩待审案件，涉及佛罗里达州据称未能充分监管化粪池系统的问题。目前，大多数已审议此类诉求的地区法院都驳回了基于劫持原则的抗辩。在我看来，它们是错误的，在此类案件中施加责任难以与最高法院的联邦主义判例相协调。

我刚刚在SSRN上发布了文章的草稿。以下是摘要：

《濒危物种法》禁止任何人——包括州和地方政府——在没有许可证的情况下“捕获”受保护物种。法院扩大了这一禁令的适用范围，对州和地方政府施加替代责任，认为政府未能充分监管对受列名物种有害的私人活动本身可能构成非法捕获。本文认为，这种“保护性劫持”与最高法院的反劫持判例无法调和。根据现行法律理论，联邦政府不得强迫州和地方政府制定或执行实施联邦法律的监管计划，也不得禁止各州对私人活动发放许可证或授权。以避免《濒危物种法》责任为条件要求各州限制私人行为，正是这些判决所禁止的行为。本文进一步认为，执行反劫持原则不必损害物种保护。《濒危物种法》的合作性联邦主义条款和现有诱导各州自愿参与的工具提供了可行的替代方案——既尊重宪法结构，又重视州和地方政府在保护受列名物种方面的实际合作重要性。

欢迎提出意见。
articles.summary:
Lights along the beach can create problems for turtles. Bright lights at night can disorient sea turtle hatchlings and prevent them from crawling into the sea. Sufficient beachfront lighting might even constitute a prohibited "take" of Loggerhead sea turtles and other turtle species listed under the Endangered Species Act.

Assume that the erection or maintenance of beachfront lighting in turtle habitat is a take under Section 9 of the ESA. Could a state or local government's decision to allow such beachfront lighting--either by permitting it or perhaps just failing to prohibit it--also be a take under Section 9? And, if so, could the ESA require a state or local government to take action against private landowners who erect or maintain beachfront lighting when sea turtles are reproducing? Would such a requirement be constitutional? I think not.

Holding state and local governments liable under the ESA for licensing, permitting, or failing to control private conduct violates the anti-commandeering principle, or so I argue in my article, "Conservation Commandeering," forthcoming in a Catholic University Law Review symposium. Nonetheless, courts impose this sort of vicarious liability on state and local governments with some frequency, most often in response to citizen suits filed under the ESA by environmental groups. Cases have involved state agency's failing to impose sufficiently stringent limitations on fur trapping or fishing, among other things. In this post I noted a case pending in the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Eleventh Circuit concerning Florida's alleged failure to adequately regulate septic systems. At the moment, most district courts to have considered such claims have rejected commandeering-based arguments. In my view, they are wrong, and imposing liability in such cases is hard to square with the Court's federalism jurisprudence.

I have just posted a draft of the article on SSRN. Here is the abstract:

The Endangered Species Act (ESA) prohibits anyone-including state and local governments-from "taking" protected species without a permit. Courts have extended this prohibition to impose vicarious liability on state and local governments, holding that a government's failure to regulate private activities sufficiently harmful to listed species may itself constitute an illegal take. This Article argues that such "conservation commandeering" cannot be reconciled with the Supreme Court's anti-commandeering jurisprudence. Under current doctrine, the federal government may not compel state and local governments to enact or administer regulatory programs implementing federal law, nor may it prohibit states from licensing or authorizing private activity. Requiring states to restrict private conduct as a condition of avoiding ESA liability does precisely what these decisions forbid. This Article further argues that enforcing the anti-commandeering principle need not undermine species conservation. The ESA's cooperative federalism provisions and existing tools for inducing voluntary state participation offer workable alternatives-ones that respect both constitutional structure and the practical importance of state and local cooperation in protecting listed species.

Comments are welcome.

The post Conservation Commandeering Is Still Commandeering appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: It Is Time To Revisit Kennedy v. Louisiana
articles.content:
我经常提及2008年6月26日，即哥伦比亚特区诉海勒案判决的那一天。但我仍然清楚记得前一天，最高法院就肯尼迪诉路易斯安那州案作出判决的日子。肯尼迪大法官宣称，存在反对对儿童强奸犯执行死刑的“全国共识”，因此第八修正案禁止这种惩罚。二十年后，我仍然对该判决感到愤慨。作为一个原旨主义者，这个判决一定是错误的，因为第八修正案的含义不能根据各州的做法而改变。而且肯尼迪没有提供任何客观依据来确定“全国共识”究竟是什么。

也许现在是重新审视这一先例的时候了。乔希·德文法官（EDMO）以肯尼迪案为出发点，认为事实情况已经发生变化。

肯尼迪案本身认为，合宪性问题会随着时间推移而改变，基于目前流行的社会“规范”。554 U.S. at 419（重点添加；引文省略）。肯尼迪明确表示，这些标准可以在短短15年内发生变化，导致不同的最终结论。Id. at 432。

今天的标准与2008年相比已大不相同。在过去三年中，至少有六个州通过了新的立法，允许对儿童强奸罪判处死刑。随着技术的最新变化，针对儿童的性犯罪率急剧上升。这些犯罪不能仅仅被描述为针对个人受害者的犯罪。这些犯罪产生的图像和视频经常在互联网上存续数十年——如果不是永远的话——因此它们不仅伤害了个人受害者，而且伤害了整个社会。最高法院在2008年认定存在反对对儿童强奸犯判处死刑的全国共识，并得出结论，最体面的做法是将死刑排除在外。但在面对针对儿童的犯罪空前增加，加上法律和技术领域的范式转变的情况下，政策制定者和检察官可能认定，最体面的做法是对那些伤害最无辜者的人施加最严厉的惩罚。肯尼迪案并不禁止这种发展。除非犯罪者满足极高的举证责任，否则最高法院的先例允许对犯有针对儿童的非致命性性犯罪的人判处死刑。

我赞赏这种方法。肯尼迪大法官编造了一个荒谬的标准，下级法院完全有权遵循。让最高法院来收拾这个烂摊子。

有人担心德文提供了咨询意见。我不这么看。这里存在真实的案件或争议：这种犯罪应如何处罚。法官是在表达他对量刑程序将如何进行的看法。法官在量刑或任何其他程序之前与律师沟通并不罕见。事实上，在量刑前会议上，法官通常会阐述他们对相关量刑指南的理解，并给各方回应的机会。深谙上诉实践的律师可能不熟悉此类审判法院的日常程序。

《是时候重新审视肯尼迪诉路易斯安那州案了》首发于Reason.com。
articles.summary:
I often speak of June 26, 2008, the day that District of Columbia v. Heller was decided. Yet, I still clearly remember the day before when the Court decided Kennedy v. Louisiana. Justice Kennedy declared that there was a "national consensus" against executing child rapists, and accordingly, the Eighth Amendment prohibits such punishment. Two decades later, I am still appalled by that decision. It has to be wrong as an originalist matter, as the meaning of the Eighth Amendment cannot change based on what states do. And Kennedy offered no objective basis to determine what the "national consensus" even was.

Maybe now is the time to reconsider this precedent. Judge Josh Devine (EDMO) takes Kennedy as a starting point, and suggests that the facts on the ground have changed.

Kennedy itself said that the question of constitutionality changes over time and is based on the social "norms that 'currently prevail.'" 554 U.S. at 419 (emphasis added; citation omitted). And Kennedy made clear that those standards can change in as few as 15 years, leading to different bottom-line outcomes. Id. at 432.

Today's standards look quite different from those in 2008. In the last three years, at least six States enacted new legislation permitting capital punishment for the crime of child rape. And following recent changes in technology, the rate of sexual offenses against children has skyrocketed. These crimes cannot accurately be described solely as crimes against individual victims. The images and videos too often produced from these crimes persist on the internet for decades—if not forever—so they harm not just the individual victims, but society as a whole. The Supreme Court in 2008 found a national consensus against capital punishment for child rape and concluded that the most decent thing was to take capital punishment off the table. But in the face of extraordinary increases in crimes against children, plus paradigm changes in the legal and technological landscape, policymakers and prosecutors may determine that the most decent thing is to impose the most serious penalty on those who harm the most innocent. Kennedy does not prohibit that development. Unless a criminal offender satisfies an extraordinarily demanding evidentiary burden, Supreme Court precedent permits imposing capital punishment on those who commit nonhomicide sexual offenses against children.

I appreciate this approach. Justice Kennedy made up a nonsense standard, and lower courts are well within their right to follow it. Let the Supreme Court clean up this mess.

There is some angst that Devine offered an advisory opinion. I don't see it. There is a live case-or-controversy: what is the punishment for this offense. The judge is offering his view of how the sentencing process will proceed. It is not uncommon for judges to talk to attorneys in advance of a sentencing, or any other proceeding. Indeed, at presentencing conferences, Judges routinely articulate their understanding of the relevant sentencing guidelines, and offer the parties a chance to respond. Lawyers steeped in appellate practice may be unfamiliar with such mundane processes at the trial court.

The post It Is Time To Revisit <i>Kennedy v. Louisiana</i> appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: New Study of Billions of mRNA Vaccine Shots Confirms Their Safety and Efficacy
articles.content:
自2020年12月特朗普第一届政府通过“曲速行动”在美国推出新冠疫苗以来，全球已接种超过130亿剂mRNA疫苗。

《柳叶刀》最新发表的一项综合综述审视了研究人员在过去六年中从这些数十亿剂mRNA疫苗接种中获得的安全性及有效性数据。该国际研究团队的这项分析纳入了随机疫苗试验、授权后监测及主动药物警戒系统的数据。

“这项综述的结论是，mRNA疫苗的严重不良反应仍然极其罕见，”研究报告随附的新闻稿指出。

一个值得注意的问题是，mRNA新冠疫苗接种会增加心肌炎（心脏炎症）的风险，尤其在年轻男性中。综述报告显示，辉瑞和Moderna的新冠疫苗心肌炎发生率分别约为每百万12.6例和35.6例。然而，新冠感染导致的心肌炎发生率比疫苗接种高出20至42倍。此外，更新版mRNA疫苗的心肌炎发生率不超过基线水平。

新闻稿进一步指出：

“分析发现，在各项临床试验和真实世界数据中，mRNA疫苗对任何已记录的新冠病毒感染的有效性约为87%，对住院治疗的有效性为93%，接种后14至42天内的死亡有效性为94%。有效性随时间推移而下降，在某些分析中，对老年人群的保护效果降低，且对奥密克戎变异株的有效性下降（对感染有效性67%，对住院有效性72%），但加强针有助于恢复保护。”

该综述还评估了mRNA疫苗技术治疗和预防其他疾病（包括流感、呼吸道合胞病毒、癌症和自身免疫性疾病）的潜力。

事实上，今年6月，美国食品药品监督管理局一个顾问委员会一致推荐一款新的Moderna mRNA流感疫苗用于50岁及以上成年人。值得注意的是，该疫苗在2月份曾被时任FDA生物制品评估与研究中心主任Vinay Prasad阻止批准。顾问委员会指出，新疫苗比传统季节性流感疫苗能引发更广泛的免疫反应，并对多种流感病毒株有效。FDA对该新流感疫苗的批准决定预计在8月作出。

值得注意的是，目前至少有120项临床试验正在开发针对各种癌症的mRNA疫苗。

总体而言，《柳叶刀》这项研究得出结论：“累积证据表明mRNA疫苗是一种安全、有效且适应性强的技术平台，对未来传染病预防和公共卫生准备具有持久意义。”
articles.summary:
More than 13 billion doses of mRNA vaccines have been administered globally since the rollout in the United States of COVID-19 shots in December 2020 via Operation Warp Speed under the first Trump Administration.

A just-published comprehensive new review in The Lancet takes stock of what researchers around the world have learned during the past six years about the safety and efficacy of those billions of mRNA vaccine shots. The analysis by an international team of researchers takes into account the data from randomized vaccine trials, post-authorization surveillance, and active pharmacovigilance systems.

"The review concludes that serious adverse side effects from mRNA vaccines remain exceedingly rare," notes a press release accompanying the study results.

One salient concern is that the mRNA COVID-19 vaccination increased the risk of myocarditis (an inflammation of the heart), particularly among young men. The review reports that the incidence of myocarditis was roughly 12.6 cases and 35.6 cases per million for Pfizer BioNTech's and Moderna's COVID-19 vaccines, respectively. However, the rate of myocarditis from COVID-19 infections was 20 to 42 times higher than from vaccination. In addition, the incidence of myocarditis from updated formulations of mRNA vaccines is no greater than the background rate.

The press release further notes:

The analysis found that across various clinical trials and real-world data, mRNA vaccines are about 87% effective against any documented SARS‑CoV‑2 infection, 93% effective against hospitalisation, and 94% effective against mortality within 14–42 days after vaccination. Effectiveness waned over time and, in some analyses, was reduced by age and against the Omicron lineage (67% against infection and 72% against hospitalisation), but booster doses helped restore protection.

The review also evaluates the potential of mRNA vaccine technologies to treat and prevent other health conditions and diseases, including influenza, Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV), cancer, and autoimmune conditions.

In fact, in June, a Food and Drug Administration (FDA) advisory committee unanimously endorsed a new Moderna mRNA influenza vaccine for adults 50 and older. Notoriously, FDA consideration of that same vaccine had previously been blocked in February by Vinay Prasad, then director of the agency's Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research. The advisory panel noted that the new vaccine provokes a broader immune response than conventional seasonal flu vaccines and exerts activity against multiple strains of influenza virus. The FDA's decision on whether to approve the new flu vaccine is expected in August.

Notably, at least 120 clinical trials are currently seeking to develop mRNA vaccines against various cancers.

Overall, The Lancet study concludes that "the accumulated evidence affirms mRNA vaccines as a safe, effective, and adaptable platform with enduring relevance for future infectious disease prevention and public health preparedness."

The post New Study of Billions of mRNA Vaccine Shots Confirms Their Safety and Efficacy appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: María Corina Machado Says Venezuela Is Blocking Her Return as Earthquake Rescue Efforts Stall
articles.content:
就在毁灭性的双地震重创委内瑞拉、造成数万人死亡、失踪或受伤仅仅数天后，反对派领袖玛丽亚·科里娜·马查多指控委内瑞拉政府阻止她和其他人入境。

这位流亡的委内瑞拉反对派领袖在地震后第二次尝试访问该国，当时她身处巴拿马，但飞往加拉加斯的航班被取消，原因是委内瑞拉政府突然关闭了领空，禁止商业航班进入。

马查多在X平台上发布的一段视频中表示：“这个政权想阻止我返回委内瑞拉，也想阻止成千上万希望前往帮助的委内瑞拉同胞回国。正如它试图阻止成千上万公民在该国分发食物和药品的慷慨工作一样，正如它阻止被困在机场的国际救援队抵达一样。”

她还指控委内瑞拉政府阻止记者进入该国，声称“当委内瑞拉人希望有尊严地安葬我们的亲人时，他们想要掩盖真相”。她声称政府已下令取消“紧急情况下所有航空公司的航班”，以阻止他们入境。

马查多的投诉并非只是一个受挫旅客的牢骚。据《华盛顿邮报》报道，委内瑞拉政府自地震发生以来一直阻碍救援工作。多支国际应急救援队被拒绝入境许可，或被政府官员中断工作。内政部长迪奥达托·卡贝洛在美国被起诉犯有毒品恐怖主义罪，他的一段视频在网络上疯传，视频中他在与一名美国急救人员争论时阻碍救援工作，当时这名急救人员正试图营救埋在废墟下的人。

此外，据《华盛顿邮报》报道，通往受灾北部各州的道路被封锁、安全检查繁复、政府索要过路费，以及严格的报道限制，都严重阻碍了委内瑞拉的国际新闻报道工作。

这种腐败行为一直是代理独裁者德尔西·罗德里格斯领导的委内瑞拉政府的标志性特征。在她的领导下，该政权延续了尼古拉斯·马杜罗时期普遍存在的对政治对手和记者的迫害。据政治犯倡导组织“正义、相遇与宽恕”称，数百名政治反对派人士仍被关押在狱中，尽管政府曾承诺释放他们。

马查多一直是罗德里格斯政权的重点打击对象。该政权拒绝为她签发委内瑞拉护照，使她实际上被困在流亡中，只能依靠外国旅行证件。2月通过的政治犯大赦法专门排除了那些因煽动针对前政府军事行动而被起诉或定罪的人，包括马查多在内。她还被禁止担任公职直至2036年，这进一步限制了她在国内的角色。

美国已承认罗德里格斯政权的非法性，美国方面表示将继续专注于提供人道主义援助并增加救灾力度，以支持这个遭受重创的国家。据《纽约时报》报道，白宫官员也对马查多越来越“感到沮丧”，因为她在地震后一再请求帮助她返回委内瑞拉，认为这是不合时宜的。

尽管面临法律障碍，马查多仍重申了她在这个危机时刻重新进入委内瑞拉的承诺。然而，鉴于罗德里格斯对权力的严密控制以及马杜罗旧政权的许多元素继续压迫委内瑞拉人民，她的期待已久的回归可能需要数年才能实现。

这篇文章首次发表于Reason.com：《玛丽亚·科里娜·马查多称委内瑞拉正在阻止她返回，地震救援工作陷入停滞》
articles.summary:
Mere days after devastating twin earthquakes rocked Venezuela and left tens of thousands dead, missing, or injured, opposition leader María Corina Machado is accusing the Venezuelan government of blocking her and others like her from entering the country.

The exiled Venezuelan opposition leader, who was on her second attempt to visit the country after the earthquakes, was in Panama when her flight to Caracas was canceled after the Venezuelan government suddenly closed its airspace to commercial traffic.

"The regime wants to block my return to Venezuela, as well as the return of thousands of fellow Venezuelans who want to go and help," Machado said in a video posted to X, "Just as it has tried to block the generous work of thousands upon thousands of citizens distributing food and medicine across the country, and just as it has blocked the arrival of international rescue teams left stranded at airports." 

She also accused the Venezuelan government of blocking journalists' access to the country, claiming "they want to bury the truth when Venezuelans want to bury our dead with dignity," and claiming that the government had ordered the cancellation of flights "of all airlines in an emergency situation" to prevent their arrival.

Machado's complaints aren't just the rants of a frustrated traveler. As The Washington Post reports, the Venezuelan government has impeded rescue efforts since the quakes. Several international emergency rescue teams have been denied entry clearance to the country or had their work interrupted by government officials. A viral video of Interior Minister Diosdado Cabello, who has been indicted in the U.S. for narcoterrorism, shows him hampering rescue efforts while arguing with a U.S. first responder, demanding that he move his car while the first responder was trying to save a person buried under the rubble.

Additionally, blocked routes and security controls into devastated northern states, instances of government extortion payments, and strict reporting restrictions have strangled international journalism efforts in Venezuela, according to the Post.

Corruption like this has been a hallmark of the Venezuelan government led by acting dictator Delcy Rodríguez. Under her leadership, the regime has continued the persecution of political opponents and journalists that was widespread under Nicolás Maduro. Hundreds of political opponents remain imprisoned by the regime despite promises of release, according to the political prisoner advocacy group Justicia, Encuentro y Perdón. 

Machado has been a major target of the Rodríguez regime, which has refused to issue her a Venezuelan passport, leaving her effectively trapped in exile and dependent on foreign travel documents. An amnesty law for political prisoners passed in February specifically excluded those prosecuted or convicted for promoting military action against the previous government, including Machado, who is also barred from holding public office until 2036, further constraining her role inside the country. 

For its part, the United States, which has recognized Rodriguez's illegitimate regime, has said it continues to focus its efforts on providing humanitarian assistance and increasing relief efforts to support the devastated country. White House officials have also grown increasingly "frustrated" with Machado for repeatedly requesting help to facilitate her return to Venezuela in the aftermath of the earthquakes, seeing it as ill-timed, per The New York Times. 

Despite the legal blocks lobbied against her, Machado has reaffirmed her commitment to reenter Venezuela at this time of crisis. However, given Rodriguez's tight grasp on power and many elements of the old Maduro regime continuing to oppress the people of Venezuela, it may take years before her long-awaited return comes to fruition.

The post María Corina Machado Says Venezuela Is Blocking Her Return as Earthquake Rescue Efforts Stall appeared first on Reason.com.
